title: Harness Config
description: Configuration for the dev-harness spec-driven development pipeline.

description.description: One-sentence value proposition for the project.

targets.description: Tool targets that `harness sync` writes output for.

paths.description: Overrides for source directory paths, relative to the repo root.

plans.description: Directory where agent plan outputs are written.

queue.description: Path to the pipeline queue JSON file.

pipeline.description: Pipeline stage definitions. Order determines agent color assignment and Copilot handoff links.

id.description: Must match the `name` frontmatter field in the corresponding agent markdown file.

readyStatus.description: Queue status value that signals this stage is ready to run.

completedStatus.description: Queue status value written when this stage finishes.

hooks.description: Configuration consumed directly by hook scripts at runtime.

extraPatterns.description: Additional shell-command patterns to block, in addition to the built-in list. Each entry is a regex string matched against the full command.

commands.description: Map of glob pattern → shell command. Run against every file written by the agent. Use `{file}` as a placeholder for the file path; if omitted the path is appended.

claude.description: Claude Code-specific settings written to .claude/settings.json by `harness sync`.

model.description: Claude model ID to pin in .claude/settings.json. Set to null to let Claude Code use the user's active selection.

permissionMode.description: Claude Code permission mode written to .claude/settings.json.

protectedBranches.description: Branch names (glob patterns allowed) that guard-destructive.mjs will refuse to push to or force-reset.

copilot.description: GitHub Copilot-specific settings used during `harness sync`.

emitChatModes.description: Kept for backwards compatibility. Has no effect since .github/chatmodes/ was deprecated in VS Code 1.106.

emitPrompts.description: Whether to write .github/prompts/*.prompt.md files (slash commands as Copilot prompts).
